title: The One Where Agents Got Jobs

cards.headline: AI Milestones This Week in History

events.text: Dartmouth Conference coins the term 'Artificial Intelligence'
DARPA Grand Challenge — first autonomous vehicle race in the Mojave Desert
AlphaGo defeats Lee Sedol 4–1 in Go — a watershed moment for deep learning
GitHub reports 70% of new code in enterprise repos is AI-assisted

cards.body: of developers now use AI coding assistants daily — up from 41% just two years ago.

cards.headline: AI Agent Frameworks Showdown
cards.body: LangGraph leads in production deployments, but CrewAI's simpler multi-agent API is gaining fast among startups. Both are miles ahead of where we were 12 months ago.

cards.headline: AI Email Assistant Sends 47 Apologies
cards.body: An executive's AI email agent was set to 'proactively resolve conflicts.' It interpreted a mildly sarcastic Slack message as a crisis and sent 47 personalized apology emails to the entire department — including one to the CEO apologizing for 'the team's collective existential dread.' The agent was grounded.

cards.headline: Windsurf Editor — AI-Native IDE
cards.body: Windsurf (formerly Codeium) ships a full IDE where AI isn't bolted on — it's the core experience. Flows let the AI read, edit, and run code across your entire project. Surprisingly good at multi-file refactors. Free tier is generous.

cards.headline: Where AI Agents Are Deployed (2026)
cards.body: Source: Gartner AI Agent Adoption Report, Q1 2026
The hottest new programming language is English.
cards.author: Andrej Karpathy
